2603.23805.title: Deep Neural Regression Collapse
2603.23805.paper_summary: Neural Collapse is a phenomenon that helps identify sparse and low rank structures in deep classifiers. Recent work has extended the definition of neural collapse to regression problems, albeit only measuring the phenomenon at the last layer. In this paper, we establish that Neural Regression Collapse (NRC) also occurs below the last layer across different types of models. We show that in the collapsed layers of neural regression models, features lie in a subspace that corresponds to the target dimension, the feature covariance aligns with the target covariance, the input subspace of the layer weights aligns with the feature subspace, and the linear prediction error of the features is close to the overall prediction error of the model. In addition to establishing Deep NRC, we also show that models that exhibit Deep NRC learn the intrinsic dimension of low rank targets and explore the necessity of weight decay in inducing Deep NRC. This paper provides a more complete picture of the simple structure learned by deep networks in the context of regression.
2603.23805.paper_summary_zh: 神經崩潰是一種現象，有助於識別深度分類器中的稀疏和低秩結構。最近的研究已將神經崩潰的定義擴展到回歸問題，儘管僅在最後一層測量該現象。在本文中，我們確立了神經回歸崩潰（NRC）也發生在不同類型模型的最後一層以下。我們顯示，在神經回歸模型的崩潰層中，特徵位於對應於目標維度的子空間中，特徵的協方差與目標的協方差對齊，層權重的輸入子空間與特徵子空間對齊，且特徵的線性預測誤差接近模型的整體預測誤差。除了建立深度NRC外，我們還顯示出表現出深度NRC的模型學習低秩目標的內在維度，並探討在誘導深度NRC中權重衰減的必要性。本文提供了在回歸背景下，深度網絡學習的簡單結構的更完整圖景。
2603.23805.author: Akshay Rangamani et.al.
